name: Fillet of Steelhead Bonne Femme
recipeIngredient: Head, tail, backbone of fish      1/3 cup White wine
1 cup Hollandaise sauce                      Cucumber and lemon slices
recipeInstructions:
-----------------------------HOLLANDAISE SAUCE-----------------------------
2 Egg yolks 1 pinch Cayenne
Salt 1/4 cup Cold butter, 8 pieces
Fresh lemon juice
This classic French dish features fish fillets served with two sauces and mushrooms in between. Fillets from small salmon, walleye, lake trout, and channel catfish are all excellent choices.
If the two sauces seem too time-consuming, this dish is still delicious using only the wine sauce.
1. Place the fish head, tail, peppercorns, and shallots in a pot with water and white wine. Bring to a boil, then simmer gently for 30 minutes. Strain and set aside.
2. Arrange the fillets in a shallow, fireproof glass or earthenware dish that has been generously buttered. Add the bouquet garni.
3. Pour in the reserved fish stock and poach at 325°F (160°C) oven temperature for 20 minutes.
4. Sauté sliced mushrooms in 2 tablespoons butter for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally until coated and golden brown.
5. Set aside the sautéed mushrooms.
6. Prepare the hollandaise sauce by whisking eggs until well blended and lemon-colored, then add a tablespoon of lemon juice when thickening begins.
7. Gradually add melted butter to the egg mixture, one piece at a time, whisking each until absorbed before adding the next. Continue until all butter is incorporated. This should take about 2 minutes; if separation occurs, add a teaspoon of cold milk or cream and continue blending.
8. Whisk in an additional teaspoon of lemon juice, a pinch of salt, and optional cayenne. Adjust seasoning to taste.
9. Assemble the dish by placing fillets on the buttered dish, covering with wine sauce, dotting with sautéed mushrooms, and pouring hollandaise over everything. Broil briefly until lightly golden.
10. Garnish with cucumber slices, lemon wedges, or watercress before serving.
